transcript.text: No problem at all.
Sorry for being a little slow to add you in there.
I saw your email a little bit afterwards.
So are you calling in from Mexico City or where in the world are you?
Yes, Mexico City.
Yep.
Been here three years, grew up here, moved back after being in Montreal, San Francisco, and Austin for about 10 years.
And yeah, we decided to land here with remote work and after COVID, it just made sense.
Yeah, totally.
I don't blame you.
I'm in Houston with my fiance, but we romanticize like going to Mexico City or something.
Her parents are from Monterey, so we're going for the first time together in December.
Oh, very cool.
That's awesome.
Yeah, it's close by.
Oh, is it?
Well, to you.
Yes.
It's closer to you.
Yes, yes.
But Mexico City is pretty far from, right?
You'd have to, like, hop on a flight.
to fly.
Yeah, for sure.
And the highways, unfortunately, are very dangerous.
So I wouldn't recommend driving.
Definitely don't plan on going far.
But great to meet you and excited to share more about GrowthX today.
I guess just maybe like a quick intro to yourself and how you heard of us would be good context to start.
Yes.
So I joined Invert about two months ago to lead marketing.
We sell to bioprocess teams.
So like in biopharma or biotech, there's a stage in the development of drugs or even food where it's like 10 years of testing and iterating on the product.
And during that stage, there's a lot of data generated.
And the teams have been working off of spreadsheets and PDFs and sending them and uploading them and between teams and between labs.
And we're offering essentially the Tableau for that.
Uh, that, um, process.
So we ingest the data, we harmonize it in real time, and then we have the analytics and AI on top of that to be able to make smarter and faster decisions.
Um, uh, we're small series A, we, um, are selling into enterprise deals, enter like big biopharma, big biotech.
Um, those deals end up, they can be like one to two year sales cycles.
We found a way to kind of cut through that with a proof of concept, a three month proof of concept that we're getting like six months to get to that, which is great.
And then we have SOW one.
Um, I come primarily from demand gen space now owning like product marketing, brand comms, and all that fun stuff as well.
Um, so my first order of business has been around like our messaging and positioning.
Um, our value props or narrative, um, and then building that brand.
And awareness, which is non-existent and, and then building that demand gen engine.
So right now the, like the sales team is fed by their own Rolodex that they come in with.
So I'm trying to find the ways to generate more eyeballs on the brand.
Like we exist, we're out there.
A lot of folks are, aren't even problem aware.
So it's like generating visibility and then to, through that generating demand through our website or a link, if they discover us on LinkedIn, what have you, but need more presence essentially.
Makes perfect sense.
Sounds like you guys are in a sweet spot.
there like direct competitors or are they sort of like tangential, either selling, you know, industry agnostic or selling, you know, this is part of like a broader suite of applications.
Yep.
So I'd say there's like smaller competitors coming up at our heels, but, uh,
But for the most part, most of our target audience is either trying their best to build it from scratch, which they've struggled a lot with, or they have piecemeals of the solution.
They have a data management thing that's kind of doing a little bit.
They have a BI tool that's kind of doing data visualization, but they don't have everything in just one place doing it all.
And they don't have anything, to your point, like it's industry agnostic.
Like they, this is where we built for bioprocess.
So like everything, everything we integrate with all of our implementation, everything is just built, designed for that and designed for managing large volumes of time series data.
Or they are just like not aware that there's a solution out there and they're just living with the pebble in their shoe.
Right, right.
And who is it from like these large enterprises that you're specifically trying to get?
Yeah.
Yeah.
I'm assuming they're on the data team, but let me hone in on the ICP a little bit better.
Yeah.
So I bucketed it into three groups.
On the executive decision maker side, especially with these big companies, we're coming in through digital and innovation.
So people who are looking for this part of their digital transformation, this is part of their AI initiative, what have you.
On the scientist side, that's the term I use, but essentially it's the actual user or manager of users or director or head of the users.
That's head of process development or the process development team or the data scientist team as well.
So that could be a process engineer as the IC.
It could be a data scientist as an IC.
It could be a scientist level two, like as an IC.
And then the last one is going to...
BIT, and they're mostly like just like part of the buying circle, but they're not the way we get into the deals.
Okay, perfect.

And the reason I asked some of these questions, like at a high level, what we're focused on is from strategy all the way to like the monitoring and analytics, helping companies create like quality at scale.
So it's SEO focused, it's GEO focused, but a lot of times our clients have a point of view on the topics they want to create content around.
And there's like a means where we can leverage AI to create the volume that you would need as like an earlier stage company.
And so I guess, you know, your company is a little more specific and niche and potentially like needing like a less is more kind of approach.
And so I sort of just wanted to get your perspective on like, you know, are you looking for something that...
It's going to help you guys create a lot more content that would be on your blog or are you looking for something that's a little different here?
Because I assume like who you're going after, it's pretty specific and folks are like more technical and also like it's massive deals probably that you guys are working on over a long sales cycle.
So yeah, I guess help me connect the dots on like whether what we are, what our bread and butter is, is connected to like what you need or if it's a little bit.
Yeah, more, more broad than that.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Well, we'd love to learn more about what you guys do because I have like a, a, a, superficial sense of it.
But I think it's like making sure we have coverage in our discoverability.
We also don't have a content engine.
So it's like, how do we, like, there is, there is a case for like thoughtful white papers that take three, four months to create and like involve talking to different stakeholders and things that.
But I think there's also like, we just need a machine happening too.
When I joined, we've never done a webinar.
We've never published an ebook or white paper.
So there's no way someone can like download or give us a lead at all on our website, apart from like requesting a demo.
And we publish a blog post like every three months.
So right now, for example, like I'm hacking my way through it with like, there's this subscription called like bear, use bear.ai.
And so I'm using them to just like generate a bunch of prompts similar to our competitors, then asking chat GPT to write the blog posts for me.
And I'm doing like maybe two a week and just like hiding them somewhere in our blog.
That's my strategy right now.
So I don't know.
So I just want to understand, like explore other solutions, other services out there.
What's what, what you guys are doing.
And, and if, yeah, we are.
The right candidate for that.
Perfect.
That sounds good to me.
I'll go through like a quick presentation and show you some examples and then we can kind of hone in on like how good of a fit it seems to be.
So, you know, at GrowthX, right, our focus is being able to address both sides of search.
So the traditional search where, you know, it's more targeting keywords, looking at SEO analysis against your competition, and then GEO, which is more audience first.
So it's understanding what are all the questions that your ICP might have given the way that you can prompt and you can basically like feed your whole life story in.
You just need to like have a larger surface area, right, to like show up and have visibility the way you want with LLM.
And so, you know, the benefit to doing so is that a lot of people are having like their first sales conversation.
And so like they get to your website, they're 4.4 times more likely to convert, they spend three times as long.
And so what our process does is we think about, you know, what are those topics where you want authority?
Who are the specific folks that you're targeting?
And how can we create content using these sophisticated AI workflows with all this context engineering that we put in, and then our experts in the loop?
And so these workflows, I'll go through one so that you have an idea of like what's happening under the hood.
But we're leveraging like, you know, all of the best models.
We're not just stuck with Anthropic or OpenAI.
We're, you know, able to like point our research to specific sources.
We're able to go really deep on the fact checking, which, right, is really important when it comes to the accuracy of the content.
And basically, we're doing this from Marcel's...
Experience of building organic content engines at every stop in his career.
So, you know, he's never believed like brands should spend a bunch on paid.
He's always believed in creating more of this content engine.

And he did that, whether it was securityintelligence.com that they made at IBM, which was their highest ROI marketing channel, or, right, like just building this team up at scale where he was the CMO leading into the acquisition by Meta.
And so, you know, with Marcel, he initially did this by building large teams.
And then at DeepGram, he experimented with these AI workflows, started doing workshops, and that's how GrowthX got started.
How did you hear of us at GrowthX in particular?
I think Marcel's LinkedIn.
Okay.
Somehow I came across it, yeah.
Yeah, totally.
So the idea is we can be end-to-end as a partner.
We're not just like an agency that is recommending strategies.
Around SEO, we're actually like staging and publishing in your CMS and being an extension of your team.
And so we do this by leveraging our own platform that has all of the workflows that we've built.
We can customize them from client to client.
And then every client that we work with, we'll get a managing director, a managing editor, and a content manager that all come from SEO and content marketing backgrounds.
And so, you know, our chief content officer was the editor-in-chief of TechCrunch for over a decade.
And so we're really just trying to combine the best of both worlds to our approach.
What is the like very tactical perspective?
Like, do you guys like have access to like our web flow and then just like automatically publish?
And like, what does the volume look like for your average client?
Yeah, definitely.
So we are working for like some pretty technical B2B clients.
Think, you know, abnormal.
Normal security.
You mentioned Webflow.
That's one of our preferred CMSs, obviously, because they're a client.
But even like, you know, Sentinel One, right, Okta, thinking about like the health space.
work with Biologica that has these women's supplements.
So we can really like get creative with the way that we build the context for these workflows and how we actually execute is we separate into two phases.
In phase one, we're generating these artifacts, which provide the context that we need.
And then we're publishing probably five to 10 articles in total.
And then in phase two, we're looking at trying to target five pieces per week.
That can be a combination of like net new and refresh.
But we will like access Webflow and basically like stage and publish on your behalf, as long as that's not a security issue.
Go
Got it.
Super cool.
I think we're too small, unfortunately.
The pricing, like at this point, I mean, our goal is next year, we want to get to Series B.
I think that might be the time for us to consider being able to afford something like this.
I see the value.
We definitely don't have the budget for something like this, but it's super cool.
I mean, this is where I would love to get to.
You just have your own like content slash like digital marketing slash like analytics team extension, you know?
Exactly.

And so what I can do is I can send you some examples, but, you know, we've built out the blog for several earlier stage companies, Town, Tiro, DataGrid, any article that you see on their blog, we've created for them.
We've also done all these guides for augment code, including like.
The image generation, there's like tables in the content, we'll do infographics as well.

And so, yeah, we can get creative and even look at like, you know, for Lovable, we're building out their templates library and making these templates more SEO optimized in terms of, you know, the use cases, the text, being able to have different insight around the different features.
But we've even done like, you know, integrations pages for Webflow, definitions that are more technical in nature.
And so, That's really cool.
Sorry, like, I just love that use case of like, not just like blog content.
It's like, you want like technical documentation if you want like compete pages or what have you.
Exactly.
You know, like we did this programmatic example for Ramp where we populated each page with the spend data.
That they had per vendor and then created these interactive charts based on the business side.
So we can definitely get more custom with the clients that we work with.
But I hear you about just wanting to raise the Series B first.
I mean, you know, to your point, like get to a place where there's a clear need for content, right?
I was kind of looking through and if it's really painful to create more than, you know, one or two pieces a month, then, you know, we can definitely be a partner that takes that off you guys' plate.
Yeah, yeah.
What is the max, like, what is the volume that you guys can, like, operate to?
Like, what does an average volume look like for your clients?
I would say daily publishing.
So we'll have five articles per week and the only thing that could slow that down is the review process.
Yeah, yeah.
Very cool.
Awesome.
If you could share with me the examples, I love it.
Like, I, if it was up to me, I would sign this, like, yesterday.
So I think this is just something that we'll have to be on our Christmas wish list, hopefully.
Yeah, no problem, Veronica.
And I'm always happy to do a demo when it makes sense.
Right now, we're in sort of this weird situation where like we can't say yes to everybody.
So definitely got to find, you know, the places where like there's a clear need with content.
There's a connection, right?
So like pipeline from being able to show up in AI Answers.
So, you know, let us know whenever the timing makes more sense.
And we can definitely do an impressive demo for the team.
Yes, thank you so much.
I really appreciate this.
Hopefully we'll chat again soon.
Yeah, hasta luego and enjoy Mexico City.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Enjoy Monterey.
Yeah, excited about it.
Thanks so much.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[GrowthX pitches its AI content service to Invert.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=542.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Invert's Challenge:** A Series A bioprocess analytics startup with no marketing engine. Sales relies on personal networks, and content is limited to a blog post every 3 months.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=167.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Solution:** An AI-powered service that acts as a full-service content team, generating 5–10 articles/week (Phase 2) and publishing directly to the CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Outcome:** Invert sees high value but is too small for the service. The goal is to revisit after raising a Series B, which would provide the necessary budget.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=842.0)

## Topics

### Invert's Marketing Challenge

  - [**Product:** Bioprocess analytics platform (the "Tableau for bioprocess") that ingests, harmonizes, and analyzes biopharma data.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
  - [**Market:** Series A startup selling enterprise deals (1–2 year cycles), shortened to \~6 months via a 3-month proof of concept (POC).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=133.0)
  - [**Problem:** No marketing engine exists.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)
      - [Sales relies on personal networks; no inbound leads.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)
      - [No content assets (e.g., webinars, white papers) for lead capture.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)
      - [Blog is inactive (1 post/3 months).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
      - [Veronica French (Head of Marketing) is using AI tools (e.g., bear.ai) to hack together 2 posts/week to build a presence.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
  - [**Target Audience (ICP):**](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=294.0)
      - [**Executive:** Digital/innovation leaders driving transformation.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=294.0)
      - [**Scientist:** End-users and managers (Heads of Process Development, Process Engineers, Data Scientists).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=294.0)
      - [**IT:** Part of the buying circle but not the entry point.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=294.0)

### GrowthX's AI Content Engine

  - [**Service:** End-to-end content creation and publishing, combining AI workflows with human experts.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=712.0)
  - [**Methodology:**](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
      - [**SEO:** Standard keyword-driven strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
      - [**GEO:** Audience-first approach that answers all ICP questions to build authority and capture AI Answer traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
  - [**Execution:**](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)
      - [**Team:** Dedicated Managing Director, Managing Editor, and Content Manager.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=712.0)
      - [**Publishing:** Direct access to CMS (e.g., Webflow) to stage and publish content.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)
  - [**Phased Rollout:**](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
      - [**Phase 1:** Generate foundational artifacts and publish 5–10 initial articles.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
      - [**Phase 2:** Scale to 5 articles/week (new + refreshes).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Capabilities:**](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=881.0)
      - [Beyond blogs → technical docs, compete pages, programmatic content (e.g., Ramp's vendor spend pages).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=881.0)
      - [Includes image generation, tables, and infographics.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=895.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler Pavlas (GrowthX):** Send Invert examples of work for early-stage clients (e.g., Town, Tiro, DataGrid).](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=881.0)
  - [**Invert:** Revisit GrowthX after raising a Series B to align budget with the service's cost.](https://fathom.video/share/eQ7aT7wL1zRqoMw_B4tPhzeNx4N3Vs37?tab=summary&timestamp=842.0)

action_items.description: Email Veronica case studies/examples + demo scheduling link
